mechanical.gap: Acceptance Criteria does not declare a red-green-revert regression guard (pattern named + what it asserts described, in the same clause)
mechanical.suggested_fix: Declare the guard in the AC, in one clause (definition-of-done.md § Bug): name the red-green-revert pattern and describe what the regression test asserts (e.g. 'fails without the fix, passes with it'). Citing the test file (e.g. a `path/to/file.test.ts` reference or a 'test: <name>' citation) is encouraged alongside the description, but a citation alone — with no description of what it asserts — is not a declaration.

manual.check: Every Acceptance Criterion is verified, with evidence cited in the completion summary (not just 'done').
Local validation passes — lint, type-check, tests (project's standard set).

Landing checks pass for the path used — PR path: all required CI checks green on the merged commit; direct-commit path (no-CI repo / meta-tooling): the project's local validation set is green, output cited (the other path is N/A by construction).
The change has LANDED on the base branch (not 'looks done') — PR path: PR merged; direct-commit path: committed to the base branch AND deployment confirmed (e.g. synced / symlinked).
The original symptom is gone, verified via the source artifact (re-test / re-run scanner) — not merely 'PR merged'.
If the fix has 3+ sibling occurrences: all swept + forward guard installed, OR a deferred sweep is documented (tracked WI, owner, target, remaining sites).

The declared regression test was actually verified by red-green-revert (pass with fix → revert → fail matching symptom → restore → pass); evidence + test path cited in the completion summary (the AC's declaration is checked mechanically — this confirms it was executed, not merely declared).

Citing the test file (e.g. a `path/to/file.test.ts` reference or a 'test: <name>' citation) is encouraged alongside the description, but a citation alone — with no description of what it asserts — is not a declaration.
